hypothesis: Fine-tuning pre-trained ML interatomic potentials (such as eSEN or eqV2) on stress tensor data selectively sampled along ML-SSNEB-converged pathways via active learning will disproportionately reduce stress prediction errors relative to energy and force errors, and this targeted stress correction will enable fully ML-converged SSNEB pathways to achieve energy barriers within 0.02 eV of DFT reference values without any DFT restart, thereby eliminating the need for the hybrid ML-then-DFT workflow entirely for materials systems with moderate structural complexity.
